description: The standard directory layout for a portable XanaNode substrate root.
root_contract: A substrate root is the directory containing substrate.json. Tools should be able to validate, pack, mount, and project a substrate from this root without needing private application state.

required_paths.description: The substrate manifest declaring id, name, namespace, version, repository, imports, sharing policy, and build/provenance metadata.

recognized_paths.description: Canonical protocol node records. A hand-built substrate may use this directory directly.
An alternate bundled protocol node artifact with a top-level nodes array.
Typed relationship records for the substrate.
Images, PDFs, audio, video, source snapshots, projection media, documents, and other files carried by the substrate.
Namespaced node type, relationship type, property, layout, or validation extensions.
Merge, compatibility, health, validation, duplicate-file, and intake reports.
Unpacked substrate packs mounted into this substrate for local federation.
Dropped-in incoming packs or source material awaiting review, mounting, importing, or merge.
Portable default author profile for hand-built or minimal substrates.
Optional RO-Crate metadata describing the substrate as a research object.

authoring_paths.description: Markdown authoring projection used by Core, Workspace, Studio, and Hugo workflows. Tools may generate protocol nodes from these files.

Local workspace metadata such as preview settings, author lists, caches, and opened-source copies. Public exports may omit private caches but must preserve metadata needed for authored workspace round trips.

archive_rules: A .substrate archive should contain every recognized path unless sharing policy excludes it.
Tools must not include .git, node_modules, public renderer output, transient caches, or generated build directories unless explicitly requested.
Every local media/source file referenced by asset_path, file, source_snapshot, primary media resolution, or registry projection metadata must be included or reported as missing.
Duplicate files should be reported by content hash rather than silently duplicated without provenance.
A hand-built substrate that follows this layout should open in Core, Workspace, Studio, Hugo, or any compatible projection without private tool state.
